page: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 40 vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 160.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a local 768 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and top-tier memory performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 10%, while multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores place in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable highlight, with both large-block read and write operations achieving top-tier performance. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations also perform in the top 25%, though single-threaded compression and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

The CPU allocation for this instance is shared, which presents a performance tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, uninterrupted 100% CPU utilization. However, this shared architecture enhances qualitative cost efficiency by offering a high density of 40 cores and 160 GB of RAM at a lower resource-to-cost ratio than dedicated instances. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage further offsets external block storage costs.

This configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, Redis caching layers, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks. The balanced memory-to-core ratio and fast local NVMe storage also make it appropriate for general-purpose web serving, data processing, and development environments that benefit from high parallelization without requiring dedicated CPU pinning.
description: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance utilizing an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 40 vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server features a shared CPU allocation and includes a bundled 768 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results show top-tier memory bandwidth for large-block reads and writes, alongside strong multi-core CPU performance in the top 25%. While the shared CPU model introduces potential resource contention under sustained peak loads, it offers a highly cost-effective resource density for multi-threaded applications. This instance is suited for database hosting, Redis caching, parallel data processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast local storage.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-40c-160gb-768s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 40 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and a 768 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core and top-tier memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-density general-purpose instance combining forty AMD EPYC cores, balanced memory, and fast local NVMe storage for parallel workloads.

160.0 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 memory-to-core ratio
768 GB bundled local NVMe SSD storage
